title: Crash: Assertion failure in t-dll-api.cc when using always_latch with constant bit selects
body:
### Description
Icarus Verilog crashes with an assertion failure when compiling `always_latch` blocks that use constant bit-select operations in the sensitivity condition.

### Environment
- **Icarus Verilog version**: 11.0 (stable)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 22.04
- **Compilation command**: `iverilog -g2012 top_2state.sv`

### Expected Behavior
The code should either:
1. Compile successfully (since constant bit-selects are valid SystemVerilog), or
2. Report a graceful error message if the feature is not supported

### Actual Behavior
Icarus Verilog crashes with:
sorry: constant selects in always_* processes are not currently supported (all bits will be included).
ivl: t-dll-api.cc:1770: unsigned int ivl_nexus_ptrs(ivl_nexus_t): Assertion `net' failed.
Aborted (core dumped)

### Minimal Reproducible Example
Please find in https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ZQ4y13OV0mpx7huTopcuCzLCHlK-KBwI/view?usp=sharing

### Compilation
`iverilog -g2012 test.sv`

### Full Test Case
If needed, I can provide the complete file that triggers this issue.
Thanks for supporting and we look forward to hearing from you as soon as possible.
